labels.name: Input
labels.desc: Data coming in from a user or external source
labels.example:
Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
String name = sc.nextLine();

labels.name: Output
labels.desc: Something printed or displayed to the user

labels.name: Variable
labels.desc: A named holder for a single value — one piece of data

labels.name: Condition
labels.desc: A decision point — code takes a different path based on a value

labels.name: Array
labels.desc: A fixed-size collection holding multiple values of the same type

labels.name: Loop
labels.desc: Something that repeats — going through each item, or running until a condition changes

labels.name: Computation
labels.desc: A calculation that produces a result — math, comparisons, or derived values

prompts.title: Salary Comparison
prompts.statement: Bob and David need to know whose annual salary is higher.

expectedTags.explanation: Two people are named, which implies two separate salary values that need to be stored — one for each person.

Someone is waiting for a result to be communicated to them — that is the intent behind output. Whenever a problem says someone needs to know or find out something, the program must print or display the answer.
A comparison must be made to determine which value is greater — that is a decision point. The result of that comparison also needs to be held until it can be shown.

prompts.title: Sandwich Shop Pricing
prompts.statement: A sandwich shop needs to let a customer know what they owe based on their order size and whether they qualify for a discount.

expectedTags.phrase: needs to let a customer know
expectedTags.explanation: The shop must communicate a result to the customer — this phrase signals that output is required before anything else is considered.
The size needs to be provided by or for the customer — it is external data that varies per transaction and drives the base price.

expectedTags.phrase: whether they qualify for a discount
expectedTags.explanation: A decision point — the final amount owed changes depending on whether an eligibility factor is met.
A price needs to be derived from the size and discount eligibility. This phrase tells us what the output contains — the calculated amount.

prompts.title: Gross Pay Calculator
prompts.statement: An employee needs to be shown their total pay for the week after entering their hours worked and pay rate.

expectedTags.phrase: after entering their hours worked and pay rate
expectedTags.explanation: Two values need to come from the user — hours worked and pay rate are provided externally, not known in advance by the program.
A value needs to be derived by multiplying hours by rate — it must be calculated and held until it can be shown.
The calculated result needs to be communicated back — someone is waiting to see it.

prompts.title: Filter Exam Scores
prompts.statement: A teacher wants to see which students in her class scored above the passing threshold.

expectedTags.explanation: Multiple students of the same type all need to be examined — a collection that must be iterated over to check each one.
Each score needs to be held as it is examined — a decision is then made per student based on whether that value meets the threshold.
The qualifying students need to be surfaced and reported back to the teacher.

codeSnippets.output:
System.out.println("Passing score: " + scores[i]);
System.out.println("Students who passed: " + passCount);

prompts.title: Student Test Average
prompts.statement: A student wants to know their average score across all of the tests they have taken this term.

expectedTags.explanation: The student is waiting for a result to be communicated — this phrase signals that output is required. Recognizing this intent early tells you the program must print or display the final answer.

expectedTags.phrase: all of the tests they have taken
expectedTags.explanation: Multiple items of the same type all need to be processed — a collection that must be iterated to accumulate a running total.
A total must be accumulated and divided by the count — the derived result needs to be held. This phrase also describes what the output contains: the calculated average.

prompts.title: Find the Area of a Circle
prompts.statement: A designer wants to know how much area a circle will cover given its radius.

expectedTags.explanation: The designer is waiting for a result to be communicated — this is the output intent. The program must display the answer once it is calculated.
A specific value needs to be provided — the radius is external data that the entire calculation depends on.

expectedTags.phrase: how much area a circle will cover
expectedTags.explanation: A value needs to be derived mathematically from the radius. This phrase also describes what the output contains — the calculated area.

prompts.title: Find the Distance Between Points
prompts.statement: A mapping tool needs to report the straight-line distance between two locations on a grid.

expectedTags.explanation: The tool must communicate a result — 'report' is a direct output signal. The program needs to print or display the answer when it is ready.
Each location requires x and y coordinates — four values that must be provided and stored to represent the two points.

codeSnippets.input:
double x1 = 3.0, y1 = 4.0;
double x2 = 7.0, y2 = 9.0;
codeSnippets.variable:
double x1 = 3.0, y1 = 4.0;
double x2 = 7.0, y2 = 9.0;

expectedTags.explanation: A value needs to be derived mathematically from the coordinate data using the distance formula. This phrase also describes what the output contains — the calculated distance.

codeSnippets.computation: double distance = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(x2 - x1, 2) + Math.pow(y2 - y1, 2));

prompts.title: Count the Days to Minutes
prompts.statement: A countdown app needs to let the user know how many minutes are in a given number of days.

expectedTags.explanation: A number of days needs to be provided — it is the starting value the entire calculation depends on.
A derived value needs to be calculated through multiple steps — days to hours, then hours to minutes — and held until it can be shown.

codeSnippets.variable:
int totalHours = days * hoursPerDay;
int totalMinutes = totalHours * minutesPerHour;
codeSnippets.computation:
int totalHours = days * hoursPerDay;
int totalMinutes = totalHours * minutesPerHour;

expectedTags.explanation: The calculated total needs to be communicated back — the user is waiting to see the result.
